'''Tex Hoy''' (born 4 November 1999) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|so}} for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]] and for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Born in [[Newcastle, New South Wales]], Hoy played his junior rugby league for the [[South Newcastle Lions]], before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]].

Hoy is the son of former semi-professional surfer [[Matt Hoy]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2019/02/14/youngster-hoy-determined-to-make-his-own-name/|title=Youngster Hoy determined to make his own name|date=14 February 2019|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

==Playing career==

===Early years===
Hoy started playing for the Knights' [[Harold Matthews Cup]] team in 2015, the [[S. G. Ball Cup]] side in 2016 and then the [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] team in 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/players/tex-hoy/|title=Tex Hoy|date=1 May 2019}}</ref> In 2019, he played the majority of the year with the Knights' [[Canterbury Cup NSW]] team.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2019/08/27/rd-24-canterbury-cup-and-flegg-teams/|title=Rd 24 Canterbury Cup and Flegg teams|date=27 August 2019|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref> In July 2019, he extended his contract with the Knights until the end of the 2021 season,<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2019/07/02/knights-extend-young-guns-hoy-and-momoisea/|title=Knights extend young guns Hoy and Momoisea|date=2 July 2019|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref> before playing for the [[New South Wales Under-20's rugby league team]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2019/07/01/nswrl-announces-cabe-under-20-squad/|title=NSWRL announces CABE Under-20 squad|date=1 July 2019|website=New South Wales Rugby League}}</ref>

===2020===
In February, Hoy was promoted to the Knights' top 30 [[National Rugby League|NRL]] squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2020/02/29/tex-hoy-promoted-to-top-30-nrl-squad/|title=Tex Hoy promoted to Top 30 NRL squad|date=29 February 2020|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref> In round 3 of the [[2020 NRL season]], he made his NRL debut for the Knights against the [[Penrith Panthers]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2020/05/26/knights-v-panthers-round-3-nrl-team-list/|title=Knights v Panthers Round 3 NRL team list|date=26 May 2020|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

===2022===
In July, Hoy signed a two-year contract with English [[Super League]] side [[Hull F.C.]] starting in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2022/07/19/hoy-to-tackle-super-league/|title=Hoy to tackle Super League|date=19 July 2022|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

===2023===
Hoy made his club debut for Hull F.C. in round 1 of the 2023 Super League season against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] which ended in a 32-30 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668525|title=Super League: Hull FC 32-30 Castleford Tigers - Away side fall just short of remarkable fightback|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> Hoy played a total of 17 games for Hull F.C. in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2024===
On 12 April, Hoy departed Hull F.C. with immediate effect. It came the day before [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]] was sacked as head coach. Hull F.C. had started the season with only one win from seven games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/tex-hoy-stance-outlined-hull-9219359/|title=Tex Hoy Stance Outlined By Hull FC|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

On 20 April, it was confirmed that Hoy had joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] until the end of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=20 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers confirm Tex Hoy signing with quota spot filled by ex-Hull FC ace |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-confirm-tex-hoy-signing-with-quota-spot-filled-by-ex-hull-fc-ace |access-date=4 May 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He made his club debut on 26 April against [[London Broncos|London]] and was voted Fans Man of the Match,<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1785636274730139987|title=The @HuntersHomes Man of the Match from Friday's game was... On debut, Tex Hoy!}}</ref> and he scored his first try for Castleford on 4 May against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 May 2024 |title=Leigh and Castleford draw in extra-time classic |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx7dyw01q66o |access-date=4 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In June, Hoy agreed a contract to remain with the Tigers for a further two years.<ref>{{Cite web |date=11 June 2024 |title=Hoy agrees two-year deal to stay with Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn00egvqvvjo |access-date=28 December 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=12 June 2024 |title='Exciting' talent Tex Hoy gives Castleford Tigers a boost by committing to the club for two years |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/exciting-talent-tex-hoy-gives-castleford-tigers-a-boost-by-committing-to-the-club-for-two-years-4662150 |access-date=28 December 2025 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref>

===2025===
Hoy began the year playing at {{Rlp|hb}} and scored a try against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in his first competitive match of the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]]. He also scored his first two goals for the Tigers, although missed a last-second conversion to level the scores, deputising for injured goalkicker [[Rowan Milnes]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 February 2025 |title=Bradford upset Cas as Salford & Giants go through |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce8jm7y7m34o |access-date=13 February 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Hoy reverted back to {{Rlp|fb}} from round 5,<ref name="RLRKC" /> and scored a try against Hull F.C. in round 6 directly from a line break inside his own half.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Woodcock |first=Ian |date=27 March 2025 |title=Hull FC claim deserved win at struggling Cas |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czxnv81ryjvo |access-date=18 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 11 against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in May, Hoy scored a hat-trick of tries and had a hand in three more of Castleford's scores.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 May 2025 |title=Hat-trick for Hoy as Cas thump struggling Salford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4ge3ewpdq7o |access-date=18 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He played a total of eighteen matches in the 2025 season, finishing as Castleford's top pointscorer as the club finished 11th on the table.<ref>{{Cite web |date=13 November 2025 |title=2025 Season Review: Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/5578/2025-season-review-castleford-tigers |access-date=28 December 2025 |website=Super League}}</ref>

In November, Castleford confirmed that Hoy had left the club to pursue playing opportunities elsewhere.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=22 November 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers confirm high-profile exit as Ryan Carr's shake-up continues |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/castleford-tigers-confirm-high-profile-1520934 |access-date=28 December 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |author=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=22 November 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers confirm Tex Hoy exit |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-confirm-tex-hoy-exit/ |access-date=28 December 2025 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> He subsequently returned to Australia.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=6 December 2025 |title=Tex Hoy sends parting message ahead of Australia return |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tex-hoy-back-sends-parting-message-ahead-of-australia-return |access-date=28 December 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

===2026===
In February, it was revealed that Hoy had signed a train-and-trial deal with [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] ahead of the [[2026 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2026/02/03/hoy-joins-manly-on-one-year-deal/|title=Hoy joins Manly on one-year deal|website=www.seaeagles.com.au}}</ref>
